defs: A peculiar substance obtained from the thyroid gland, containing from nine to ten per cent of iodine.
notes: ☞ It is a very stable compound, and is believed to be active principle in thyroid extracts and in the internal secretion of the thyroid gland. It was originally called thyroiodin.
☞ It is probably an impure extract of thyroid gland, containing thyroxine. See Merck Index 11th Ed. #9345, the entry for thyroidin.
